name: Mascarpone
recipeIngredient: 1 tbsp White wine vinegar (or lemon juice)
recipeInstructions:
Makes About 1 Pound
1. Choose a stainless steel bowl that fits inside a large saucepan without touching the bottom of the pan.
2. Add water to the saucepan and place the bowl in so it touches the surface of the water but sits firmly on the rim.
3. Place the bowl on medium heat and bring the water to a boil.
4. Place the cream in the bowl over the boiling water, adjusting heat to medium.
5. Heat the cream, checking temperature often with an instant-read thermometer until it reaches 190°F; stir occasionally.
6. Stir in the vinegar and continue stirring gently until the cream begins to curdle.
7. Remove the pan from the heat, cover, and allow the curds to firm up for 10 minutes.
8. Line a strainer or colander with dampened cheesecloth, napkin, or coffee filters.
9. Place the strainer over a bowl and carefully spoon the curds into it.
10. Allow the mascarpone to cool to room temperature, cover the strainer tightly with plastic wrap, and refrigerate for 24 hours to allow the cheese to finish draining and become firm.
11. Store in the refrigerator in a tightly covered container. Use within 3-4 days.
